reviews.review_description: This game could be awesome but is super clunky. Great tunes, but no feedback on whether you're hitting the beats or not. Any updates happening?

The gameplay is just broken. The drum mode doesn't respond to the hits correctly, the other game modes just sucks, you can literally just let the notes come to your hand without moving. It has potential to be a good game, but for now it just sucks. Don't waste your $10 bucks
I got as far as the song selection screen.  I select a song, choose a difficulty level, click play, and nothing happens.  Can’t even get to the actual game.

reviews.review_title: Some tweaks and it' ll be a great game!
reviews.review_description: Love the playlists, and it's a generous list of songs for an indie game so cheap! I wasn't expected the dual beat saber / rock band gameplay, that was brilliant! But guys, you need to seriously tweaks the hitboxes. Floating boxes are too small and / or drumsticks are too short. I can finish the songs if I hit the boxes with my fists instead of the game's drumsticks, and it's not comfy.

reviews.review_title: Music so cool. Hit box so small
reviews.review_description: They have added a no fail option.  I can only assume I’m not playing correctly because I miss so many notes.  Please make the sticks longer or wider or something.  I get thru 20% of a song on easy and fail. So no fail it is but missing every other note just kills the enjoyment for me.   Will gladly bump up to 4 stars if this is fixed

The targets are too small, and the batons often pass right through them without registering any points. Hitting the targets with your fists works almost every time, but that’s probably not the intended way to play.
The game itself is really fun, and the music is wonderful. There’s definitely potential for future DLCs.
I’d love to give it 5 stars, but please fix these issues quickly.
Yes, I know it’s early access.
For now, 3 stars as encouragement — and I’ll be happy to update it to 5 in the future.

POSITIVES 
Love the music and the 15 track previews as well as the general look and feel before starting the actual game. It’s promising that it has three different variations of games. It’s also a good price too. 

NOT SO POSITIVES 
I didn’t realise I sucked so badly at rhythm games. I’m not bad at smash drums, beat saber, synth riders or many others I’ve tried. All three of these are just way too quick for this middle age punk fan. 

This game is in desperate need of some options. I can’t finish any level even on easy. If I could slow down my Q3 maybe that would help. Some sort of basic tutorial could too. After around 14 minutes I was not making much progress. I tried 4 or 5 songs but they were all ridiculously quick. At the very least the game needs a no fail option. If I could listen to whole tracks, I’d probably keep it, but the previews are unfortunately the only good thing for me at the moment. I don’t know if there are plans to improve it, i hope so. I have loads of fun games and with this one I just don’t find it fun at all. 

At the moment I’ll give it a very generous 3 stars. One for introducing me to some new quality punk bands that I will certainly try and listen to more. Another for the intro and initial feeling I got when I first loaded it up. The final one as I hope it maybe improved in the future.

developer_response.body: NO FAIL option? Fine with us. Look for the update soon.

reviews.review_description:
How the hell does this game have this few ratings and why is it so cheap?
The songs are so well choreographed it actually feels like you‘re playing the song. 
The optics are not as clean and polished as Beat Saber, but I had even more fun with it. 
I can only assume that the Fempunk aesthetics put off potential buyers but the songs actuly kick butt. 
Totally  love it!!

This game brings a fresh punk energy to the rhythm genre, delivering an experience that feels like Beat Saber but with its own unique twist. With three different modes, there's plenty of variety to keep things exciting.

The atmosphere is fantastic, and the soundtrack is simply outstanding—every song is engaging, diverse, and makes you want to move. Plus, the visuals are top-notch, with solid video clips that add even more style to the gameplay.

If you love rhythm games and want something with attitude, energy, and pure fun, this is a must-play. Time to boogie!

Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
